Satellite Intelligence

Dubai Nighttime Lights: Crisis Impact

Satellite detection of urban activity decline following the Feb 28 – Mar 2026 Iranian missile strikes

VIIRS DNBNASA Black MarbleMapboxCRISIS DETECTION

March 29, 2026  |  Data: Jan 1 – Mar 25, 2026

March vs Feb
(lunar matched)

(wk 3-4)
21
March daily
observations
1,155
Hex cells
mapped
Verified finding: All 400 urban hex cells (baseline >20 nW) show a mean -12.1% decline in March. All 8 nightlife zones declined -14% to -18%, worsening to -23% to -27% in late March. Low-density desert/peripheral areas show apparent “increases” — this is sensor noise in near-zero readings, not real activity growth. The urban decline correlates precisely with Iranian missile strikes (Feb 28+), airspace closures, and expat evacuations.

Interactive Impact Map

709 urban hex cells (Feb baseline > 5 nW) colored by % radiance change. Red = decline, Gray = stable, Green = growth. Low-density areas shown as faint outlines (noise). Click any cell for details.

News & Events Correlated with Satellite Data

Complete timeline of strikes, airspace closures, evacuations, and satellite radiance readings

Date Category Event Radiance vs Feb Source
PRE-CRISIS BASELINE
Jan 1-31BaselineNormal operations. Avg daily radiance 31.8 nW31.8—Satellite data
Feb 1-27BaselineNormal operations. Avg daily radiance 31.7 nW31.7—Satellite data
CRISIS BEGINS — INITIAL STRIKES
Feb 26-27MILITARYUS-Israel coordinated strikes on Iran’s military & nuclear infrastructure36.6+15%Wikipedia
Feb 28STRIKEIran retaliates: 247 ballistic missiles + 230 drones at UAE, Bahrain, Qatar. Shahed-238 drone hits forecourt of Fairmont The Palm. Debris near Burj Al Arab. UAE airspace partially closed.37.4+18%Bloomberg
WEEK 1 — AIRSPACE SHUTDOWN & INITIAL CHAOS
Mar 1FLIGHTSUAE airspace fully closed. All commercial flights suspended. ~20,000 flights cancelled across Middle East. Debris hits Dubai Airport.33.7-10%Al Jazeera
Mar 2FLIGHTSLimited flights resume evening. UAE reports 174 missiles tracked (161 intercepted), 689 drones (645 intercepted), 8 cruise missiles.37.8+19%Fortune
Mar 3STRIKEOngoing strikes. UAE intercepts total reaches 357 missiles, 1,800+ drones. US/UK/DE/FR/IN/CN carriers halt all Dubai flights.41.7+32%Wikipedia
Mar 5FLIGHTSflydubai resumes reduced schedule. Most international carriers still grounded.37.1+17%TimeOut Dubai
Mar 7ANOMALYMajor satellite anomaly. Radiance drops 44% — possible blackout measures or cloud event.17.7-44%Satellite data
WEEK 2 — EXODUS & EVACUATIONS
Mar 9-12EVACUATIONMass expat exodus. Companies evacuating staff. “Functioning but tense.” No satellite data available (coverage gap).——CNBC
Mar 13RECOVERYCNBC reports: “Those that stayed say life is functioning but tense.” Restaurants & beach clubs less busy.29.4-7%CNBC
Mar 14FLIGHTSEmirates expands schedule. Air Canada cancels all flights through May.32.2+2%TravelPirates
WEEK 3 — SECOND WAVE & CONTINUED DISRUPTION
Mar 16STRIKEDrone hits fuel storage tank near DXB airport. Massive fire. Flights suspended for hours.30.7-3%CNBC
Mar 17FLIGHTSUAE airspace fully closed 3-5 AM. Reopened. HRW condemns Iran strikes as “unlawful, endangering civilians.”30.0-5%HRW
Mar 18RECOVERYAirspace reopened. Emirates & Etihad back in air. Operating ~60% of pre-war capacity.29.7-6%TravelPirates
Mar 19ANOMALYExtreme satellite drop — 72% below baseline. Possible blackout event or thick cloud.9.0-72%Satellite data
Mar 21ANOMALYSecond major radiance drop.16.9-47%Satellite data
WEEK 4 — SUSTAINED DECLINE, NO RECOVERY
Mar 23SECURITYUS Embassy issues security alert. Level 3: Reconsider travel. Routine consular services suspended.20.9-34%US Embassy
Mar 24STATUSCumulative: 398 missiles, 1,872 drones, 15 cruise missiles intercepted. 11 killed, 178 injured.24.0-24%Wikipedia
Mar 25LATESTLatest satellite data. No recovery visible. Nightlife zones remain 20-27% below baseline.25.4-20%Satellite data
Mar 26SECURITYSecond US Embassy security alert issued.——US Embassy
Mar 28STATUSEmirates aiming to restore full operations by Mar 29, subject to security. Tourism crisis deepens.——Nomad Lawyer

Methodology & Sources

Satellite Data

EOG VIIRS Monthlyv10, 500m, Jan-Feb 2025/2026
NASA VNP46A2Daily BRDF-corrected, Jan-Mar 8
NASA VNP46A1Daily at-sensor DNB, Jan 1-Mar 25

Controls

LunarPhase-matched (new moon <10%)
CloudQF_Cloud_Mask <40%
SpatialH3 res-7 (~1.2km²)
